name: Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop
name_original: Vegadeo y Alto de Garganta – circular desde Taramundi
description: Tackle the difficult Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop, a 41.6-mile road cycling route with 5302 feet of elevation gain through Asturian mountai
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Taramundi traverse a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, idyllic valleys, and lush green forests. The region, nestled in Asturias, offers varied topography with challenging climbs and scenic descents, often on paved surfaces. Numerous rivers and streams contribute to the verdant scenery, providing a dynamic environment for cyclists. This area provides a range of routes suitable for different fitness levels, from moderate rides to more demanding ascents. Best road cycling routes around Taramundi The most popular road cycling route is Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop, a 41.6 miles (66.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Taramundi and La Bobia Loop, a difficult 36.5 miles (58.7 km) path. This route offers varied topography with challenging ascents and descents through the region's valleys and hills. Local road cyclists also love the Porto Bridge – Hornos de Vilaoudriz loop from Taramundi, a 40.9 miles (65.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Taramundi is defined by mountainous terrain, idyllic valleys, and lush green forests, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Taramundi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Taramundi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi offers a diverse selection of over 40 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate rides to more demanding ascents through the region's mountainous terrain and valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: Road cycling in Taramundi features varied topography, from challenging climbs to scenic descents. The routes primarily use paved surfaces, winding through mountains, idyllic valleys, and lush green forests. You'll encounter dynamic environments with constantly changing vistas, often alongside rivers and streams.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Taramundi?
A: Yes, Taramundi has routes suitable for beginners. While many routes offer significant elevation, there are options designed for a more moderate experience. For instance, you can find routes with up to 500 feet of elevation gain, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Taramundi offers several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069239541/alto-de-la-bobia-oscos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 41.6-mile (66.9 km) trail with over 1600 meters of elevation, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353793696/taramundi-and-la-bobia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taramundi and La Bobia Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.5-mile (58.7 km) path featuring over 1700 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: Many of Taramundi's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porto Bridge – Hornos de Vilaoudriz loop from Taramundi&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through river valleys, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bres Village – Vegadeo loop from Taramundi&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi's routes are rich with natural beauty. You'll cycle through lush forests, alongside rivers like the Turia and Cabreira, and experience varied mountainous terrain. A notable natural highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing stop. The region's 'Water Route' also showcases the historical use of water in the area.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to visit while road cycling in Taramundi?
A: Absolutely. Taramundi is known for its rich cultural heritage. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazonovo Mill Museum&lt;/a&gt;, the largest museum of windmills in Spain, or explore the Ethnographic Ensemble of Teixois. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín (Taramundi)&lt;/a&gt;, with its 16th-century altarpieces, and the Taramundi hillfort also offer glimpses into the area's history.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about cycling in Taramundi?
A: The road cycling experience in Taramundi is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, which combine challenging climbs with scenic descents through mountains, valleys, and forests. The well-preserved traditional villages and cultural sites also add to the unique appeal.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi's lush green forests and rivers make it appealing throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, with vibrant foliage in autumn. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially when cycling through shaded forest sections, though some climbs might be more demanding in warmer weather.

Q: Are there options for food and accommodation near the road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: Yes, Taramundi offers various options for food and accommodation. The charming villages you'll pass through, such as As Veigas, often have local eateries. The old rectory, La Rectoral, converted into one of Spain's first rural retreats, provides impressive views and is a perfect stop for cyclists. You'll find places to refuel and rest throughout the region.

Q: Is public transport available to reach road cycling routes in Taramundi?
A: While Taramundi is a rural area, local bus services connect some of the larger villages. However, for direct access to specific route starting points, especially in more remote areas, personal transport or cycling directly from your accommodation is often the most convenient option. Parking is generally available in and around the main villages.
tips: Beautiful bridge that crosses the river Era uniting the communities of Galicia and Asturias.

Driving along the AS-11 highway we come across the town of Folgueiras, about 7km from Vegadeo. It is located at 342 meters above sea level and in winter times it will surely await us with a strong wind. In the same town we will find a source to fill the bottle.
Driving through the Asturian council of Taramundi we found ourselves in this beautiful town. It's about Bres, a small parish that offers great views.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It features significant climbs, especially the Alto de la Bobia, which is known for demanding ascents with sections reaching double-digit gradients, up to 20% in some parts. Expect sustained climbs averaging 10% over several kilometers, requiring very good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road surface like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characterized by green and lush Asturian mountains with deep valleys. While the road surface is generally in good condition, some initial sections might be narrower and rougher. Expect rugged, somewhat bumpy asphalt in places, which can affect your speed. Higher sections are more exposed to wind.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers scenic views throughout the Oscos region. Notable points include the Porto Bridge, the charming settlement of Folgueiras, and Bres Village. As you ascend the Alto de la Bobia, you'll be rewarded with panoramic vistas across the green mountain tops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route is accessible from towns like Taramundi or Vegadeo. It's advisable to look for public parking in these larger settlements before starting your ride. The region is relatively isolated, so traffic is minimal, making roadside parking in appropriate spots a possibility, but always check local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is generally during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn. This avoids the harsher winter conditions and ensures more pleasant temperatures for tackling the significant climbs. Be prepared for varied weather, as higher elevations can be exposed to wind.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route or to enter the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve. The area is open for public enjoyment, but always respect local regulations, private property, and the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de la Bobia - Oscos Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on public roads, so while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not a typical 'dog-friendly trail' in the hiking sense. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and not interfering with traffic or other cyclists. Always follow local leash laws and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the loop overlap with the "Road Oscos, Cuenca del Navia y Sierra de Penouta" (30%) and sections of the "[CIMA AS32] La Bobia * Vegadeo" (28%) and "[CIMA AS32] La Bobia * Taramundi" (14%). You'll also briefly encounter parts of "Mtb Taramundi" and "Ruta de los Molinos".

faqs.question: What makes the Alto de la Bobia climb particularly notable?
faqs.answer: The Alto de la Bobia is renowned for its demanding and sustained gradients, often featuring in 'top climbs' lists for serious road cyclists. It includes sections with double-digit gradients, reaching up to 20% on the concrete section of the Arruñada wall, making it a true test of endurance and strength.

faqs.question: What kind of natural environment can I expect in the Oscos region?
faqs.answer: The Oscos region, part of the Río Eo, Oscos, and Terras de Burón Biosphere Reserve, offers stunning natural beauty. You'll cycle through typical Asturian landscapes with green, lush mountains, deep, closed valleys, and extensive vegetation. The area is known for its unspoiled natural environment and panoramic views from higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: The Oscos region is relatively isolated with few inhabitants, especially in the deeper valleys. While specific cafes directly on the route are not guaranteed, you might find small shops or cafes in the villages you pass through, such as Folgueiras or Bres Village. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.
